what_it_does: Acceptance test for the L9 reverse journey-recording feature: it exercises a Suite API to start a journey, append ordered ui:// addresses, stop, retrieve, and replay the path, while checking that malformed addresses fail loud and the existing review-session organ remains unchanged and distinct.
observations: Uses a temporary FsStore and NodeRegistry discovered from the nodes directory.
Defines a global PASS counter and a check(label, cond) helper that asserts and tallies.
Tests the full journey lifecycle: start_journey returns an id and open state, append_journey_step adds addresses, stop_journey finalizes.
Verifies the recorded steps preserve order and each step carries address plus ts.
replay_journey returns an ordered addresses list plus the journey id for frontend stepping.
Malformed step addresses raise an exception and are not persisted.
Appending to an already stopped journey raises an exception.
The review-session organ is exercised with start_session, present_current, and next to confirm unchanged behavior.
Explicitly asserts that a journey id cannot be loaded as a session, emphasizing separate stores/objects.
standouts: Reverse journey recording modeled as a first-class, parallel object separate from review sessions.
S0 grammar gate for ui:// addresses reused via parse_ui_address on every appended step.
Fail-loud behavior for both malformed addresses and writes to finalized journeys.
A direct distinctness check between journey and review-session identifiers/stores.
conventions: Follows an inline acceptance-test script style with assertions and a pass counter.
Uses tempfile.mkdtemp and shutil.rmtree for isolated store cleanup.
Adopts the ui:// address scheme and an open step dict convention containing address and ts.
Breaks strict exception specificity by catching bare Exception to detect any raised error.
concerns: Catches generic Exception, so the tests may pass if an unrelated error is raised.
Relies on Suite methods whose implementations are not visible in this file.
Uses a mutable global PASS counter, which is unusual for a test module.
The review-organ test builds a small node graph inline, mixing journey testing with unrelated graph setup.
notes: An acceptance script that turns the L9 journey-recording design into a checklist of observable Suite behaviors, with a guard that the review session organ is left untouched.
questions: What exact exception types should be raised for malformed addresses and closed journeys?
How is journey storage implemented so that load_session cannot retrieve a journey id?
What is the precise S0 grammar for ui:// addresses that parse_ui_address enforces?
How does replay_journey feed the ordered addresses back into the forward resolver?
purpose_vs_actual: matches — the file’s stated purpose is to prove reverse journey-recording behavior and preserve the review organ, and the code does exactly that.
apparent_themes: Separation of concerns between navigation recording and review sessions.
intention_signals: Introduce a distinct journey-recording object that captures free UI address traversal, validates addresses with the existing S0 grammar, supports ordered replay, and does not overload or alter the review-session organ.
novelty: A parallel reverse-journey recorder that is intentionally not a review session, with its own lifecycle, replay vocabulary, and explicit distinctness guard.
contribution: An executable acceptance specification for a journey-recording API, covering start/append/stop/get/replay, S0 address validation, error handling for malformed and finalized journeys, and a regression guard for review-session behavior.
summary_for_embedding: An acceptance test script that validates a Suite-based reverse journey-recording API for ordered ui:// address paths, S0 grammar gating, and review-session isolation.
intention_for_embedding: To prove that a new journey-recording object captures, retrieves, and replays ordered navigation paths without affecting the existing review-session organ.

symbols.does: Asserts a condition, increments the global pass counter, and prints an ok message.
symbols.description: A small local assertion helper used throughout the acceptance script. It takes a label and a condition, fails with that label if the condition is false, otherwise increments PASS and prints a success line. It is the only top-level deterministic symbol defined in this file.
